name: dmatrix_squadrack
_comment: d-Matrix SquadRack full-rack reference, modeled at chiplet granularity (8 servers × 8 cards × 8 chiplets = 512 chiplets). Per chiplet: 32 GB LPDDR5 capacity memory at 51.2 GB/s (= 2 channels × 25.6 GB/s per Fig 5 of the d-Matrix Corsair whitepaper Nov 2024); 256 MB on-die SRAM 'performance memory' at 18.75 TB/s (= 2 GB / 8 chiplets per card; 150 TB/s aggregate / 8 chiplets, sram.md §3.2); 300 TFLOPS MXINT8 dense (= 2400/8 per card; 1200 TFLOPS MXINT4 per chiplet). SRAM is modeled as a first-class fast tier via the sram_*+hbm_* convention (sram.md §1.1 path 1) — get_tiers() materializes [SRAM tier, HBM-slot tier] where the 'hbm' slot here represents the LPDDR5 main memory. Performance Mode tuners (placement=auto/auto) put weights+KV in SRAM when capacity allows; Capacity Mode (weights_tier='hbm') pins weights to LPDDR5 to free SRAM for KV / larger context. Fabric chain: (1) `pair_mesh` — 16-chiplet all-to-all domain spanning a paired pair of Corsair cards (DMX Link D2D within each package + DMX Bridge across the two cards, per whitepaper Figs 6/9), modeled as a full mesh; per-chiplet outbound 64 GB/s (DMX Bridge 512 GB/s shared across 8 chiplets per card; 1 TB/s D2D within package is faster but the cross-card share dominates for collective-style traffic), 0.115 μs D2D latency. (2) `pcie_server` — 4 pair-domains per 8-card server connected via 2× 144-port PCIe Gen5 switches (whitepaper Fig 10); 64 GB/s per chiplet (PCIe Gen5 x16 single-direction), 0.65 μs PCIe switch latency. (3) `ethernet_rack` — 8 servers per rack via 2× JetStream NICs per server + standard TOR ethernet switches (1 JetStream per PCIe switch, JetStream brief Sept 2025 Fig 2). Each JetStream is 400 Gbps = 50 GB/s single-direction, PCIe Gen5 x16 host (overprovisioned vs the 50 GB/s wire); per-server aggregate = 100 GB/s. The 'rank' at this tier is the whole server (8 ports = 8 servers per rack), so per-port BW = 100 GB/s — the dispatcher's hierarchical decomposition handles the per-chiplet chunking via payload telescoping (M·L/N on outer phase). 2.0 μs transparent-NIC latency (Hot Chips 2025). INC structurally absent on the entire chain (no SHARP-class hardware on JetStream/standard TOR path, mesh has no switch ASIC). Inter-rack scale-out fabric not modeled. Sources: d-Matrix Corsair Technical Whitepaper (Nov 2024), JetStream Product Brief (Sept 2025), Hot Chips 2025 coverage.
